{{Quote|Our differences make us strong, I've learned that.|Dorsk 81 to [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]]|<ref name="Champ">''[[Champions of the Force]]''</ref>}}
'''Dorsk 81''' was a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] of the [[New Jedi Order]], and one of the 83 [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] from the [[Dorsk]] lineage. He was also a member of the inaugural class of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] [[Jedi Praxeum]]. Dorsk died in [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]] after giving himself completely to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] in an effort to stop [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala|Daala]] from destroying the Jedi Praxeum.

==Biography==
===Upbringing===
{{Quote|I am the eighty-first clone of Dorsk. Eighty generations before me have been identical, doing the same jobs with the same level of skill, maintaining our level of perfection and not slipping back—but I was a failure, I was different.|Dorsk 81|<ref name="Apprentice" />}}
He came from the planet of [[Khomm/Legends|Khomm]] where, circa a [[1000 BBY/Legends|thousand years before Dorsk 81 was created]], the [[Khommite/Legends|Khommites]] had believed that their genetic structure was perfect and had frozen evolution by creating [[Cloning/Legends|clones]]. Dorsk 81, however, was slightly different from his ancestors, in his mind and spirit and his ability to allow [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to flow through him. Because of this difference, Dorsk 81 felt that he was a failure to his race, until he heard that the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] was rebuilding the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].<ref name="Apprentice" />

===Jedi Knight===
{{Quote|Next time I will be stronger.|Dorsk 81 at the Jedi Praxeum|<ref name="Apprentice" />}}
[[File:Dorsk81b.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Dorsk 81 watches as [[Gantoris]] duels [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]].]]

When [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] set up his [[Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], Dorsk 81 went to see him to seek guidance. Dorsk decided to stay and became one of the first twelve trainees that Luke taught. The diversity of the jungle world was bewildering for the sheltered man, but he slowly learned to cope with it, forming friendships with the other trainees such as [[Witches of Dathomir|Dathomiri witch]] [[Kirana Ti]] and the young [[Kyp Durron]]. Dorsk struggled somewhat with the Force and was nearly injured one evening in an underground cavern. While floating in a mineral spring with the other Jedi, he panicked when a bubble of super-heated vapor began to rise towards the trainees. Only the intervention of [[Gantoris]] enabled Dorsk to divert the heat. Dorsk again saw this strength of Gantoris' used in a more sinister way during a duel between Gantoris and Luke Skywalker. It was also Dorsk who fetched [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] Skywalker when the charred body of Gantoris was found.<ref name="Apprentice" />

At one point, he journeyed into the jungle with Kyp to the [[Temple of Exar Kun]]. There, Kyp put him to sleep while he learned from the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]]. [[Exar Kun/Legends|Kun]] wanted to kill the clone but Kyp prevented this. Durron later betrayed the Jedi and fled the Academy in [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade's]] [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunter]].<ref name="Apprentice" /> Dorsk 81 worked with the other students to defeat Exar Kun and later forgave Kyp, realizing he had been under the sway of the Sith Lord.<ref name="Champ" /> After he was [[Knighting Ceremony/Legends|knighted]], he left the Academy to become a [[Jedi Watchman/Legends|Jedi Watchman]] of Khomm.

[[File:Dorsk81_and_Kyp.jpg|thumb|200px|Dorsk 81 and [[Kyp Durron]] discover [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun's]] temple in the jungle.]]

Khomm, however, was not open to this role, expecting Dorsk to slot back in to his old roles. Unable to accept this, or to integrate his new knowledge and experience, Dorsk left soon after with Kyp to investigate the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]]. There, they stumbled upon a resurgent [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[United Warlord Fleets|faction]]. At a hyped-up rally, they discovered this group was led by none other than [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala|Daala]]—against whom Kyp had gone on a personal crusade and believed to be dead. In a loud outburst, their presence was discovered at the rally and they had to flee quickly.<ref name="Darksaber" />

Having identified Dorsk 81 as a Khommite, [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] and Daala decided to add Khomm to their list of targeted worlds. Dorsk tried to warn his people and predecessors, but they refused to believe him. Many clones were destroyed in the assault on Khomm.<ref name="Darksaber" />

===The ultimate sacrifice===
{{Quote|They're gone, my friend.|Dorsk 81 to Kyp Durron|<ref name="Darksaber" />}}
Fleeing back to the Jedi Academy in a stolen Imperial [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]], Dorsk 81 and Kyp Durron arrived just in time to warn a group of thirty gathered students of an [[Assault on Yavin 4|impending attack]]. Daala and Pellaeon were determined to wipe out the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] as a symbolic victory.<ref name="Darksaber" />

[[File:Dorsk81c.jpg|thumb|left|160px|Dorsk 81 listens as [[Tionne Solusar|Tionne]] plays "[[The Ballad of Nomi Sunrider]]" on her [[Double viol/Legends|double viol]].]]

Fortunately, the students were not at the [[Great Temple/Legends|Great Temple]], but rather at the [[Temple of the Blueleaf Cluster/Legends|Temple of the Blueleaf Cluster]], which confused the Imperials into designating this as their primary target. Fighting against the first wave of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]], Dorsk 81 hurled giant rocks at the ships using the Force and caused them to crash. The other students did likewise, repelling the first wave of TIE fighters and [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|bombers]].<ref name="Darksaber" />

Recognizing their vulnerability in the jungle, Kyp called a retreat to the main temple. There they fought off a ground assault force, Dorsk again using the Force to good effect and knocking an [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]] into a jungle tree. In the short reprieve between assault waves, the Jedi gathered for a planning session in the [[Strategy Center|War Room]]. Aware of the seventeen [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyers]] in orbit, Dorsk suggested using the Force to thrust them away, based on Yoda's axiom "Size matters not", used by Luke Skywalker as one of the most important teachings in his New Jedi Order.<ref name="Darksaber" />

Arranging the Jedi on the outside points of the Grand Temple, Dorsk stood alone on the observation deck, atop a Force apex. All the Jedi channeled their power through him, and surrendering fully to the Force, Dorsk shoved the Star Destroyers end-over-end out of the [[Yavin system/Legends|star system]] in what possibly is the most powerful use of [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force Push]] in recorded history. The enormous Force power flowing through him overpowered his body, burning through it and killing him. As Kyp raced up the temple to catch Dorsk, he uttered his final words: "They're gone, my friend".<ref name="Darksaber" />

===Legacy===
[[File:Dorsk_81_Death.jpg|thumb|200px|Dorsk 81's death.]]
{{Quote|Only Dorsk 81 had the bravery and the skill to save the jungle moon… He drew upon everything, opened every cell of his body to the Force—and unleashed it!|[[Kyp Durron]] recounts Dorsk 81's death|<ref name="Leviathan" />}}
The removal of the Star Destroyers left only the ground forces to mop up. The Jedi did this easily, aided by the return of Luke Skywalker and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. Admiral Daala arrived in her ''[[Knight Hammer]]'', but shortly after, Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]] arrived with a New Republic fleet that defeated her.<ref name="Darksaber" />

Dorsk 81 was later [[Burial/Legends|buried]] in a grove in the Yavin jungle, with a gray plinth marking his grave. His was the second plinth placed there, Gantoris was the first, but in time it would be joined by many others.<ref name="Darksaber" />

The destruction of the Khommite society and the heroic sacrifice of Dorsk 81 shocked the Khommite people out of their complacency and into a reform of their ways. Dorsk 81 was deified by them, and his successor, [[Dorsk 82]]<ref name="Victory">''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest]]''</ref> would go on to study at the Jedi Praxeum. Dorsk 82 struggled with coming to terms with the legacy of Dorsk 81 when Kyp Durron gave him Dorsk 81's lightsaber,<ref name="Leviathan" /> but eventually, he too gave his life as a sacrifice to protect civilians during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]].<ref name="Victory" />

==Personality and traits==
Dorsk 81 was a kind being. He was selfless, as shown by his sacrifice to save the Jedi Praxeum. Dorsk was friends with Kyp Durron, who was much more reckless than Dorsk. When he traveled back to his homeworld after becoming a Jedi Knight, Dorsk 81 was surprised by the complacency of the Khommite people. He desired to change things for them, and to make them willing to fight for a cause. Ultimately, Dorsk 81 was able to come to peace with the Force and himself, and was willing to give his life to save others.

==Behind the scenes==
In The New Essential Chronology Dorsk 81 is mistakenly said to have been killed twice, on Yavin 4 and then again on Ando. The Dorsk who died on Ando was actually Dorsk 82, his clone.
